finding: Different properties of objects (color, state, exemplar) are forgotten independently over time rather than as bound units, indicating distributed non-unitary memory representation.
Negative behavior (greed) propagates more than positive behavior (generosity) through indirect reciprocity chains; this asymmetry is driven by negative affect rather than incentive magnitude.

Testing (retrieval practice) can impair subsequent free recall when it depletes relational processing resources, but improves cued recall where cue-target relations are diagnostic, showing effect direction depends on task demands.

Deception detection accuracy increases markedly when judges are distracted from conscious deliberation, because unconscious processing integrates complex cue information bottom-up without top-down stereotyping biases.

Words with consonants articulated front-to-rear are preferred over rear-to-front words; effect occurs via covert motor simulation and is absent in patients lacking subvocalization, indicating embodied metaphor from deglutition kinematics drives preference.

Good deeds motivated by religious obligation are perceived as less moral than identical deeds motivated by secular choice, because religious motivation reduces attributed responsibility by making helping appear a side effect of obligation rather than primary goal.
